content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 10, 2006

James R. Sutton
The Sutton Law Firm
150 Post Street, Suite 405
San Francisco, CA 94108

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-095

Dear Mr. Sutton:

The purpose of this correspondence is to confirm that since we have not received word from you since our last correspondence, we have withdrawn your request for advice.

In a telephone conversation with Kevin Heneghan, of the Sutton Law Firm, it was brought to our attention that your advisee may no longer need the requested advice. In our correspondence dated June 16, 2006 (copy enclosed), we advised that unless we received word from you by June 30 we would be withdrawing your request for advice. Pursuant to this correspondence, we have withdrawn your request for advice.

However, if at a later date you determine that you do desire advice, you may simply request it anew at that time.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
BGL:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-095

sections.question_synthetic: No specific legal question was posed; this document addresses a procedural withdrawal of a previously submitted advice request.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice pursuant to their prior June 16, 2006 correspondence, as no response was received from the advisee by the June 30 deadline, and a phone conversation indicated the advisee may no longer need the requested advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter with no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document confirms withdrawal of a previous request for advice based on procedural deadlines and lack of response from the requestor.

QUESTION: No specific legal question was posed; this document addresses a procedural withdrawal of a previously submitted advice request.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice pursuant to their prior June 16, 2006 correspondence, as no response was received from the advisee by the June 30 deadline, and a phone conversation indicated the advisee may no longer need the requested advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms withdrawal of James R. Sutton's advice request (File No. W-06-095) due to lack of communication by the June 30 deadline and indication that the advisee may no longer need the advice. The FPPC advises that a new request can be submitted if advice is needed in the future.
